description_long: This is a lush, conical evergreen Christmas tree with dense, layered branches that extend outward in a natural, slightly irregular pattern. The branches are covered in green needle-like foliage, with some brownish undertones visible on the inner and lower parts, mimicking the look of a real pine or fir tree. The tree trunk is thick and dark, visible at the base and partially obscured by the dense foliage. There are no visible decorative ornaments, string lights, or a topper on the tree, giving it a natural, undecorated appearance. The tree stands upright and is full-bodied, with branches that create a wide base and taper smoothly to a pointed top. The overall appearance is realistic and would fit well in urban parks, plazas, or as a seasonal display.
description: A full, natural-looking evergreen Christmas tree with dense green branches and a thick trunk, lacking any visible decorations or topper. The tree has a wide base and tapers to a pointed top.
description_view_0: The tree is seen from the front, showing a wide spread of branches and a clear view of the thick trunk at the base.
description_view_1: This view highlights the left side, with branches appearing slightly denser and the trunk less visible.
description_view_2: The tree is viewed from the back, with branches overlapping and the trunk mostly hidden by foliage.
description_view_3: The right side is visible, showing a balanced spread of branches and a subtle view of the trunk at the base.
